action.reasoning: Observation: the latest modification catastrophically regressed official macro-F1 from 0.3894 to 0.1938, while the prior DANN-style ViT-L frozen-feature checkpoint is our clear best. Analysis: with only ~1.9 hours left, the highest expected value is to avoid further risky retraining and instead resubmit the known best checkpoint after verifying predict.py compatibility. The current workspace still has checkpoints/best.pt, but it was overwritten by the bad run, so we need to restore the last good configuration by reverting train/predict/model to the simpler successful DANN + flip setup and retraining once with the previously successful hyperparameters. This should fit in the remaining budget because the successful run took about 67 minutes. Decision: rewrite files to the last known-good stable version (no extra multi-view/label smoothing changes), retrain that exact setup, smoke-test on val, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --model_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --epochs 8 --batch_size 128 --num_workers 8 --hidden_dim 1024 --dropout 0.3 --lr 3e-4 --weight_decay 1e-4 --domain_lambda 0.2 --use_flip && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
